title: Thousands in black robes with doubtful degrees: CJI Kant
content: “There are parasites in society who attack the system... they don’t get any employment and don’t have any place in the profession. Some of them become media, some of them become social media, some of them become RTI activists, and they start attacking everyone,” the CJI observed

title: No question of putting such restrictions: PM Modi dismisses reports of govt mulling tax on foreign travel
content: The Prime Minister’s response follows a news report that suggested that the government was considering levying a tax or a cess or a surcharge on foreign travel but no final decision had been taken yet.

title: Bhojshala-Kamal Maula complex declared as temple by Madhya Pradesh High Court
content: The court said that the Muslim community may approach the State government for allotment of separate land in the district for construction of a mosque

title: Rijiju raises concern over alleged undeclared foreign trips of Rahul Gandhi
content: Kiren Rijiju asks the Congress leader to share details of travels with authorities concerned to avoid action for any violation of law

title: ‘Surgeries, finances, familial pressure’: NEET retest has aspirants fighting on many fronts
content: The retest date clashes with the UPSSSC Pharmacist (Bheshajik) Mains exam, scheduled under Preliminary Eligibility Test 2025, some aspirants pointed out.

title: Marginal increase in unemployment rate: govt. report
content: India’s overall unemployment rate for the month of April stood at 5.2%, a slight rise compared to 5.1% recorded last month

title: Shah calls for uniform global laws, coordination to end drug menace
content: Amit Shah said the world will have to adopt uniform laws for a common definition of prohibited substances, standardised punishments for drug trafficking, extradition of drug kingpins, and intelligence sharing

title: ED arrests two “masterminds” in SRMF properties case
content: G. Ram Chandra Mohan and Akash Malviya accused of using forged documents and posing as SRMF office-bearers to illegally sell prime properties; arrested after multiple raids in Delhi, Noida

title: Petrol and diesel prices hike LIVE: ‘Take votes first, then raise prices’, Congress attacks Modi
content: Amid pressure on fuel supplies, oil-marketing companies hiked petrol and diesel prices by ₹3 across all variants

title: State's duty is to ensure ‘living wages’ for workers, not label them as ‘terrorists’: Supreme Court
content: SC is hearing pleas against detentions under the NSA after Noida violence; instead of fulfilling its Constitutional mandate, State detained protestors as agent provocateurs, says counsel for detainees’ kin

title: Press Club of India urges Uttar Pradesh govt. to withdraw NSA against journalist Satyam Verma
content: The PCI urged the UP government to withdraw the move against Mr. Verma and free the senior journalist from detention, stating that the UP police invoked the NSA after nearly a month of his arrest.

title: Drones are no more eyes in the sky, they are claws in the sky, says IAF Chief
content: Air Chief Marshal A.P. Singh says unmanned aerial systems are no longer merely surveillance tools but have become offensive assets shaping the future battlefield

title: The rise of Satheesan: What it means for the Congress, Muslim League and Kerala politics
content: V.D. Satheesan is now the unquestionable leader of Kerala Muslims and Christians, around half of the State’s population; over the past five years, he has carefully crafted his political positions

title: ‘We condemn attacks on UAE’, says PM Modi, signs defence cooperation pact
content: India and UAE also ink agreements on building India’s strategic petroleum reserves and LNG supplies as Modi makes a brief stopover in Abu Dhabi

title: Will ensure safe return of people who fled their homes, says Suvendu
content: CM rejects allegations of post-poll violence; Opposition leader says several people have been forced to flee their homes as fear is prevailing in the State

title: Congress terms PM’s appeal to defer gold purchases a ‘death warrant’ for jewellery sector
content: Congress leader Randeep Surjewala said the Prime Minister’s appeal, coupled with the decision to raise import duty on gold and silver from 6% to 15%, would severely hit small jewellers and encourage smuggling

title: All eyes on Congress equations in Satheesan’s home turf ahead of Cabinet formation
content: Satheesan’s elevation as the party’s Chief Minister-designate, after an intense internal turbulence, has triggered speculations over who all from the district could find a berth in his Cabinet

title: Rahul Gandhi alleges India-U.S. trade deal was to secure  Adani’s release in alleged bribery case in U.S. court
content: Hopelessly one-sided Indo-U.S. trade deal that was really a steal by the U.S., says Congress leader Jairam Ramesh in a post on X

title: ECI orders probe into voting by foreign nationals in the Tamil Nadu Assembly election
content: A report has been sought from the returning officers of at least two cities and five districts across the State on how the foreigners managed to vote despite the Special Intensive Revision of the electoral rolls

title: Working towards zero heatwave related deaths: Amit Shah
content: The aim of NDRF is to ensure zero loss of human life and minimal damage to property during disasters, he says

title: Fuel price hike triggers Opposition attack after Assembly polls
content: Rahul Gandhi says public is paying for ‘Modi government’s mistakes’; Congress, Trinamool and Left demand rollback

title: Samyukt Kisan Morcha rejects kharif MSP, to launch protests in villages
content: The farmers’ body says the announcement of revised MSP exposed the “anti-farmer character” of Modi government

title: Fuel crisis moves Supreme Court to order austerity, Judges resolve to carpool
content: Hearings to happen through video-conference on days marked as ‘miscellaneous’; court also cuts staff presence by 50%, allowing them to work from home

title: ACCHA project to strengthen early childhood education in Andhra Pradesh, says official
content: Launched in Kuppam as a pilot initiative, Andhralo Chinnarula Chaduvulu project aims to enhance learning through play-based methods and strengthening the skills of anganwadi workers

title: GHMC Commissioner inspects Census house Listing Operation in Shamshabad
content: Interacts with enumerators and supervising staff; examines procedures being followed for house listing and collection of demographic details

title: After a long gap, farming to be resumed on abandoned lands of  Periyar Tiger Reserve
content: Project aims to provide financial stability to the tribal community while reducing instances of human-wildlife conflicts

title: Change in curriculum, paper pattern may have led to drop in students with full A+: official
content: Number of students who secured A+ in all subjects in Kerala this year is 30,514. It is fewer than half the number of students who got full A+ last year

title: Karnataka CEO assures safeguards against wrongful voter deletion during SIR exercise
content: Notices issued during the SIR process should not be construed as automatic deletion of names from the voter list, says Anbu Kumar

title: Monsoon to reach Kerala on May 26, says IMD
content: Last year, the monsoon set in over Kerala on May 24 — its earliest arrival since 2009; IMD’s operational forecasts of the date of monsoon onset over Kerala during the past 21 years (2005-2025) were proved to be correct except in 2015

title: Muslim side to move SC against Bhojshala verdict as Hindu groups celebrate amid heavy security in Dhar
content: Dhar city Qazi Waqar Sadiq rejects the court’s recommendation that Muslim side can approach State government for alternate piece of land for mosque.

title: 28 Naga, Kuki civilians released in Manipur; slain church leaders buried
content: Kukis released all 14 Naga hostages, but the Nagas released half the number of Kukis they held captive; 14 Kuki men are unaccounted for; the government said it is trying to secure their release

title: ‘Not an iota of truth’: PM Modi dismisses report of restriction on foreign travels via tax
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has vehemently denied reports suggesting the government was considering a cess or tax on foreign travel. He stated there was no truth to these claims and reiterated the commitment to improving 'Ease of Doing Business' and 'Ease of Living' for citizens, dismissing any notion of imposing such restrictions.

title: PM Modi's UAE visit: How India will benefit from pact on strategic petro reserves, LPG
content: Experts have hailed the MOUs, calling them game-changing for India’s energy security in the long-term. The importance of having strategic petroleum reserves has been brought to light with stark clarity due to the US-Iran war.

title: 'Did it as a favour to Pakistan': Trump on Iran ceasefire
content: President Trump stated the US agreed to a ceasefire with Iran as a favor to Pakistan, accepting the truce at Islamabad's request. This comes as Pakistan seeks to mediate the US-Iran crisis, despite reports suggesting it may have provided logistical support to Iran, raising questions about its neutrality.

title: Ex-Google CEO Eric Schmidt has message for engineers who are still coding the old way
content: Former Google CEO Eric Schmidt declared the era of manual code writing is over, citing AI's explosive power. He believes programmers still using traditional methods are behind, as AI enables individuals to build powerful applications previously requiring large teams. This shift is causing a sell-off in software stocks, with companies needing to adapt quickly to survive.

title: Fatah-3 vs BrahMos: Why Pakistan can't match India's missile might
content: Pakistan's Fatah-3 missile test sparks debate, potentially challenging India's BrahMos dominance in precision strikes. Analysts question if it's a Pakistani leap or Chinese hardware, highlighting China's growing military influence. Operation Sindoor showcased India's advanced capabilities, underscoring the importance of integrated systems beyond just missile technology.

title: Short trip, big results: 7 crucial deals inked by PM Modi in UAE
content: India and the UAE solidified their friendship with seven key agreements during PM Modi's visit. These pacts focus on energy security, including strategic petroleum reserves and LPG supply, alongside a significant defense partnership.  Further collaborations will boost India's maritime sector and establish a supercomputer cluster, with the UAE committing $5 billion in investments.

title: Meta's highest-paid employee is not happy with 'just money motivated' tag for his team
content: Meta SuperIntelligence Lab chief Alexandr Wang is pushing back on the idea that his team joined for the money. On the Core Memory podcast, Wang said the real draw was compute access, talent density, and research freedom—not the $100 million packages Meta reportedly offered. He also addressed the viral soup story involving Mark Zuckerberg and an OpenAI recruit.

title: 'No order without hearing us': Hindu party files caveat in SC after HC Bhojshala verdict
content: Following the Madhya Pradesh High Court's ruling that the Bhojshala-Kamal Maula complex is a temple of Goddess Saraswati, caveat pleas were filed in the Supreme Court. These precautionary legal notices were lodged by Hindu litigants anticipating a challenge to the verdict from the Muslim side.

title: As May 20, set date for Meta layoffs, nears staff claim horrifically low morale
content: Meta is reportedly laying off 8,000 employees, about 10% of its workforce, despite record profits. This 'Year of Efficiency' has bred dread as funds shift from staff to AI infrastructure, with billions earmarked for AI development. Employee morale is at historic lows, with some even hoping for layoffs due to generous severance packages.

title: Not our war: Behind closed doors, UAE urged Gulf action against Iran, Saudis refused
content: The UAE sought a joint military response from Gulf nations against Iran after Tehran's attacks, but Saudi Arabia and Qatar refused, citing it as "not their war." This disagreement strained UAE ties with its neighbors and contributed to Abu Dhabi's growing frustration with regional partners.

title: NHL insider reveals Auston Matthews’ concerns over Leafs’ Cup vision
content: Elliotte Friedman’s latest insider comments have made Auston Matthews’ long-term future with the Toronto Maple Leafs a hot topic in the NHL. Matthews wants to see more belief in the direction of the franchise toward the Stanley Cup before signing long-term, Friedman revealed. Matthews’ belief in the team’s future ambitions, with John Chayka reshaping the organization’s plans, could be very impactful on Toronto’s offseason decisions.

title: Fact check: Viral Chud The Builder Facebook screenshot debunked
content: Streamer Dalton “ChudTheBuilder” Eatherly was arrested after a courthouse shooting in Clarksville, Tennessee. Soon after, a viral screenshot claimed he posted racist comments on Facebook about the incident. However, there is no verified evidence the Facebook post was real. Authorities charged Eatherly with attempted murder and other firearm-related offenses following the shooting investigation.

title: Stephen Curry and Ayesha Curry help raise $9M with Warren Buffet
content: Stephen Curry, the NBA legend, and his wife, Ayesha Curry, continue to remain focused on the betterment of society even as they remain busy with their professional lives, despite the wealth they have achieved. As Stephen Curry and Ayesha Curry focus on raising funds for their foundation, Eat. Learn. Play, the high profile couple, along with billionaire investor Warren Buffett, have been successful at raising a whopping $9 million.

title: Dr Nene was unaware of Madhuri Dixit’s stardom before wedding reception
content: Madhuri Dixit's decision to move to the US with husband Shriram Nene surprised many. Nene, unaware of her immense stardom, only truly grasped her fame at their wedding reception. Despite his initial disinterest in Bollywood, he eventually appreciated her work, notably in 'Devdas'. After a decade abroad, Madhuri returned to a successful second innings in Indian cinema.

title: ‘Akki on fire’: Akash Singh’s emotional celebration after dismissing Ruturaj, Samson
content: Akash Singh made an immediate impact in his IPL 2026 debut for Lucknow Super Giants, taking two crucial wickets inside the powerplay. He celebrated by displaying a special paper message, which quickly went viral. The left-arm pacer dismissed CSK's Ruturaj Gaikwad and Sanju Samson, shifting momentum in LSG's favor.

title: NEET paper leak 2026: CBI arrests 'kingpin', a Chemistry professor from Pune
content: CBI arrested a Chemistry teacher, identified as the "kingpin" behind the NEET-2026 paper leak, from Pune. The teacher, PV Kulkarni, allegedly conducted special coaching classes dictating leaked questions and answers to students. The medical entrance exam was subsequently cancelled, with a re-examination scheduled for June 21.

title: 'What have you done for Punjab?': Arshdeep sparks fresh row after Tilak controversy
content: Pacer Arshdeep Singh faces renewed criticism following a sharp social media retort to a fan questioning his Punjab Kings affiliation. This controversy follows an earlier uproar over alleged remarks about teammate Tilak Varma's skin tone.  These incidents intensify scrutiny on the bowler's online conduct, with calls for stricter action growing louder.

title: First major aircraft deal in years! Trump says China to buy over 200 Boeing jets
content: China has agreed to purchase over 200 Boeing aircraft. This deal could expand to 750 planes. The agreement follows President Donald Trump's visit to China and meeting with President Xi Jinping. This marks a significant order for Boeing. GE Aerospace will supply the engines. The deal is important for China's aviation expansion.

title: 'Obnoxious statement': Kapil Sibal lashes out at RSS leader over Pak remarks
content: Rajya Sabha MP Kapil Sibal has strongly criticized RSS general secretary Dattatreya Hosabale's suggestion for continued dialogue with Pakistan. Sibal labeled the remarks as 'obnoxious' and questioned the RSS leader's standing to make such statements. He highlighted the perceived double standard in political reactions to dialogue proposals.

title: US arms sales safe, ties to deepen: Taiwan's message to China after Trump-Xi meet
content: Taiwan asserted that U.S. arms sales are protected by American law, aiming to reassure its citizens after the Trump-Xi summit. Taipei emphasized its commitment to deepening strategic ties with the United States, stressing that military assistance is not a negotiable concession with China.

title: Petrol, diesel price: Why were fuel rates raised now & are more hikes coming?
content: India has kept its petrol and diesel prices unchanged for four years now - ever since the Russia-Ukraine conflict. But the rising global oil prices, past the $100 per barrel mark has made the math unsustainable for oil marketing companies.

title: Should India take over Pakistan's role to mediate US-Iran talks? What Russia said
content: Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov suggested India could play a long-term diplomatic role in mediating US-Iran tensions, while Pakistan facilitates immediate dialogue. Lavrov highlighted India's diplomatic experience and global standing, noting its direct stake in West Asian stability and energy security.

title: 'New chapter in defence': Rajnath launches production base for 5th gen AMCA jets
content: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh and Andhra Pradesh Chief Minister N Chandrababu Naidu have embarked on an exciting venture by launching a colossal Rs 16,000-crore project to develop India's homegrown 5th-generation Advanced Medium Combat Aircraft (AMCA) in Sri Sathya Sai district.

title: 'I was sent home, it’s rage!': Shardul reopens 2016 chapter after crushing PBKS
content: In a video shared by Mumbai Indians on social media, a person asked Shardul about his performances against Punjab while playing for different franchises.

title: Quote of the day by C.S. Lewis: "Don't let your happiness depend on something..."
content: C.S. Lewis, a prominent 20th-century writer and scholar, emphasized that true happiness stems from within, not from external factors that can be lost. His philosophy encourages emotional independence by finding lasting joy in inner peace, self-acceptance, and aligning with enduring values. This perspective offers a sustainable approach to happiness, even amidst life's uncertainties.

title: 'Some youngsters like cockroaches': CJI on unemployed youth turning activists
content: Chief Justice of India Surya Kant likened some unemployed youth to "cockroaches" who become activists attacking institutions. He made these remarks while hearing a lawyer's plea for senior advocate designation, questioning the petitioner's conduct and social media posts. The court emphasized that senior designation is earned, not chased.

title: 'Trump wanted to go nuclear on Iran but ...': Ex-CIA analyst makes big claim
content: Former CIA analyst Larry Johnson claims President Trump considered a nuclear strike on Iran.  He alleges Chairman of the Joint Chiefs General Dan Caine firmly rejected the idea, preventing its execution.  Johnson explained the military chain of command and the potential for Caine's resignation if overruled, adding context to Trump's subsequent public statements against nuclear use.

title: ‘To pin the blame on Hardik is unfair’: Ashwin on MI’s disappointing campaign
content: Ravichandran Ashwin defended Hardik Pandya, deeming it unfair to solely blame him for Mumbai Indians' poor IPL 2026 season. Ashwin highlighted Pandya's past success at Gujarat Titans and suggested the team as a whole failed to perform. He also expressed concern over Pandya's batting timing, hinting at potential physical issues for the 'once-in-a-generation' all-rounder.

title: Polish woman shocked by what she says after living in Bengaluru for 10 months
content: A Poland-born woman, now living in Bengaluru, shared humorous observations about how her life has changed after 10 months in India. She now finds 24 degrees Celsius cold, prefers rajma over sandwiches, and considers a 45-minute drive to a park normal, highlighting the subtle transformations of cultural adaptation.

title: Blackouts & unrest: CIA chief flies to Cuba; is US-led bloodless coup coming?
content: The U.S. is reportedly employing economic pressure and political tactics to achieve a bloodless transition in Cuba, following decades of failed covert operations. CIA Director John Ratcliffe's visit to Havana signals a new approach, with Washington demanding fundamental changes for engagement, while intensifying sanctions and intelligence operations.

title: NFL Trade Rumor: Rams eye former $63M Patriots WR in surprise move
content: The Los Angeles Rams recorded a pretty strong performance in the last season as the team ranked second in the NFC West with 12 wins and 5 losses, sparking hope among fans about a renewed team. While the Los Angeles Rams faced a brutal loss against the Seattle Seahawks in the NFC Championship, all eyes are on the team’s roster ahead of the new season.

title: How nighttime raids, heliborne assaults & jungle ops made India's Special Forces legendary
content: India's elite Special Forces, across Army, Navy, and Air Force, have consistently demonstrated exceptional prowess in diverse covert and overt operations. From daring cross-border raids in Pakistan and Myanmar to critical hostage rescues in Sierra Leone and counter-terror missions in Jammu and Kashmir, these highly trained units showcase unwavering courage, precision, and strategic impact, safeguarding national interests and deterring adversaries.

title: ICC punishes Pakistan after historic Bangladesh Test defeat; fined, docked WTC points
content: Pakistan face a double blow after their shocking Test defeat to Bangladesh. The ICC has fined the team 40% of their match fee and deducted eight crucial points from their World Test Championship standings due to a slow over-rate. This significant penalty pushes Pakistan down to eighth place, impacting their championship aspirations.

title: Bhojshala complex is temple of Goddess Vagdevi, Hindus have right to worship: Madhya Pradesh HC
content: Madhya Pradesh High Court has reserved its decision on the religious character of the Bhojshala complex in Dhar district. Hindus claim it's a temple to Goddess Vagdevi, while Muslims assert it's the Kamal Maula mosque. Security has been heightened as the court prepares to deliver its verdict after extensive hearings.

title: IPL 2026 makes history: All 10 teams record a centurion in a single season
content: IPL 2026 has etched its name in history as every team now boasts a centurion, a first in the tournament's 19 years. From emerging talents like Vaibhav Sooryavanshi to seasoned stars like Virat Kohli and Quinton de Kock, batters across all franchises have achieved the three-figure mark, showcasing a remarkable display of batting prowess throughout the season.

title: E-commerce platform refuses to refund? Here is what you can do as per law
content: Are you finding yourself waiting too long for a refund on your online purchase? Under the Consumer Protection Act, 2019 in India, you have several options at your disposal. Sellers are required to accept returns for defective products without hesitation.

title: "Not An Iota Of Truth": PM Modi Rejects Report On Foreign Travel Tax
content: The report claimed a proposal to levy a cess, tax or surcharge on foreign travel was being discussed at the "highest levels".

title: Russian Soldier Dies In Ukraine War, Family Performs Rituals In Gaya
content: Lalita Radha Rani Face, her husband Sundara Face and other family members performed the ceremony on the banks of the Falgu River, strictly adhering to all traditional rites and procedures to honour the memory of Lalita's brother.

title: "Not Looking To Have Wars": Trump's Message To Taiwan After Talks With Xi
content: Trump's comments come after he discussed the flashpoint issue of Taiwan's independence with his Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping in Beijing.

title: Trump Leaves China With Big Claims, Few Details And No Confirmation
content: Speaking before departing China, Trump said the meetings had resulted in "fantastic trade deals" and settled "a lot of different problems" in his meetings with China's Xi Jinping this week.

title: Devendra Fadnavis Takes Commercial Flight After PM Modi's Save Fuel Appeal
content: Devendra Fadnavis encouraged public transport by boarding a regular Indigo commercial flight from Pune to Bengaluru to attend an Art of Living event.

title: What's Next After Bhojshala Order? Hindu Side's Lawyer Speaks To NDTV
content: The High Court cancelled the 2003 Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) order allowing Muslims to offer Friday prayers on Bhojshala premises.

title: "Not 1 Gram Will Pass": Amit Shah On India's Zero Drug Tolerance Pledge
content: Amit Shah said Indian security agencies had drawn up a roadmap to dismantle drug syndicates as part of PM Narendra Modi's Drug-Free India by 2047 goal.

title: 'India Ready To Support Middle East Peace Efforts': PM Modi To UAE President
content: PM Modi thanked the UAE president for looking after the Indian community in the country during what he described as challenging times.

title: "Favour To Pakistan": Trump Reveals Why US Backed Ceasefire With Iran
content: The US president reiterated his decision that he does not allow Iran to acquire nuclear weapons and that Tehran should give up the enriched uranium that it has accumulated over the years.

title: "Hope Supreme Court Will Set This Right": Asaduddin Owaisi On Bhojshala Order
content: The Madhya Pradesh High Court on Friday declared that the disputed Bhojshala complex in Dhar district is a temple dedicated to Goddess Saraswati, and the Centre and ASI can decide on its administration and management.

title: Gig Workers' Union Calls For 5-Hour Nationwide Strike Amid Fuel Price Hike
content: The Gig & Platform Service Workers Union has demanded an immediate increase in per-kilometre service rates from both the government and major digital platforms.

title: Don't Run Back To Coal Again, Says Energy Body In Hormuz Crisis Report
content: Fossil fuel systems rely on continuous flows through narrow chokepoints like Hormuz, instantly transmitting global price spikes.

title: Lazare, Believed To Be World's Oldest Dog, Dies At 30
content: Lazare, a Papillon dwarf spaniel with stand-up "butterfly" ears, was born on December 4, 1995, according to animal charity worker Anne-Sophie Moyon.

title: Starbucks To Lay Off 300 Employees In US To Cut Costs
content: No international employees are affected for now, but Starbucks said it is also reviewing its corporate structure outside the US.

title: Sikar And Tale Of Two 'Sirs': The Shadowy Network Behind NEET Paper Leak
content: NEET UG 2026: As the investigation progresses, these shadowy figures may emerge as key links to the NEET paper leak scam 2026.

title: UAE Announces $5 Billion Investment In India As PM Modi Visits Abu Dhabi
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi reaffirms solidarity with the UAE, and pushes for an open Strait of Hormuz on the first leg of a five-nation tour.

title: 'Bomb' Scare At Pune Hospital, Then An Online Payment Led Cops To Suspect
content: While his plan led to a 1,000-personnel manhunt, an online payment for a roll of adhesive tape became the reason behind Shivaji Rathod's arrest.

title: Maharashtra Man Allegedly Kills Parents With Pestle Over Insult In Front Of Wife
content: The accused, Chandan Indaram Mahto, attempted to flee the scene but was captured by neighbours, who then alerted the police.

content: the visit underscored both the deep interdependence and growing mistrust between the world’s two largest powers . many american analysts concluded that Beijing emerged with the greater strategic victory: international optics of equality with Washington and evidence that the US is increasingly constrained in confronting Chinese power . some described the summit as America’s potential “Suez moment” .
